title: neolitsea gilva
intro: Neolitsea gilva, also known as the Indian boxwood or yellow boxwood, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Lauraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Neolitsea gilva include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Neolitsea gilva can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, and are also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote digestion and alleviate coughs.
